qa_pairs.question: Why is the annual renewal of Alliance user accounts necessary?
qa_pairs.answer: The annual renewal of all user accounts is essential to collect up-to-date information required by our granting agencies and to deactivate accounts that are no longer in use.

qa_pairs.question: Who is required to renew their Alliance account, and when?
qa_pairs.answer: All Alliance users must renew their account between May 6 and June 9, 2026.

qa_pairs.question: What happens if I do not renew my Alliance account by the specified deadline?
qa_pairs.answer: If you do not renew your account, it will be automatically deactivated after June 9, 2026. This means you will not be able to access your data, but it will not be deleted immediately.

qa_pairs.question: Can a deactivated Alliance account be renewed at a later time?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, you can renew your account at any time after it has been deactivated.

qa_pairs.question: Do recent Alliance account holders need to complete the renewal process?
qa_pairs.answer: If you created your account after January 1, you do not have to renew it at this time.

qa_pairs.question: What is required of recent Alliance account holders who are exempt from renewal?
qa_pairs.answer: Recent account holders still have to accept the new policies and consents, which will prompt on CCDB the first time they try to log in.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I view the Privacy Policy and other agreements I have accepted for my Alliance account?
qa_pairs.answer: All agreements that you are required to accept or indicate your preference have to be accepted on CCDB. Once signed, you can see them in the 'Agreements' page (https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca/agreements/user_index).

qa_pairs.question: What types of agreements are available on the CCDB 'Agreements' page?
qa_pairs.answer: The agreements include Policies, Standards, Terms, and Procedures; Consent to Access User Data; Optional transfer of file ownership on account deactivation; and Consent to receive optional news and communications from the Digital Research Alliance of Canada.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find the official Alliance policies?
qa_pairs.answer: The policies are available on the Alliance website (https://alliancecan.ca/en/policies).

qa_pairs.question: What defines a primary role in an Alliance account, and what is its purpose?
qa_pairs.answer: Your primary role must correspond to your main position, generally the one you will hold for the longest. It helps manage accounts with multiple roles, facilitates communication, and aids in reporting.

qa_pairs.question: What happens if I fail to renew my primary role during the annual renewal period?
qa_pairs.answer: If you do not renew your primary role during the annual renewal period, your account will be deactivated.

qa_pairs.question: Are new roles automatically set as primary by default?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, when new roles are activated, they are automatically set as the primary role by default.

qa_pairs.question: Can users with multiple active roles change their primary role?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, users with multiple active roles can change their primary role at any time on CCDB's 'Home' page by selecting the 'Make this role primary' checkbox next to their preferred role.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if my Alliance role has changed, for example, moving to a new faculty position or transitioning from a Master's to a PhD?
qa_pairs.answer: If your role has changed, you must apply for a new role. You can do this by logging into CCDB and going to 'My Account' → 'Apply for a new role'.

qa_pairs.question: How should I request multiple roles if my situation requires them?
qa_pairs.answer: If you need several roles, please request one role at a time.

qa_pairs.question: If a new role is approved, is it automatically set as my primary role?
qa_pairs.answer: Any new role approved is set as your primary role by default. If you do not wish to have a new role set as your primary role, you should uncheck the 'Make this role primary?' box.

qa_pairs.question: What action should I take if my current Alliance role(s) are no longer needed after a change in my situation?
qa_pairs.answer: If your current role(s) are no longer needed, please click on 'disable old roles'. This will leave your new role as the primary one and deactivate any other role you have.

qa_pairs.question: My Alliance account has been deactivated, but I need access to my data to move it off the Alliance Federation clusters. What should I do?
qa_pairs.answer: Please send an email to support@tech.alliancecan.ca so that we can assist you with your request.

qa_pairs.question: Who should I contact if I can't find the answer to my account renewal question in this FAQ?
qa_pairs.answer: If you don’t see the answer to your account renewal question in this FAQ, please send an email to renewals@tech.alliancecan.ca for assistance.

qa_pairs.question: What are the consequences for a faculty member if they do not renew their Alliance account by the deadline of June 9, 2026?
qa_pairs.answer: Any active account that is not renewed between May 6 and June 9, 2026, will expire and be automatically deactivated after that date. Additionally, if the faculty member sponsors other users, their account and the accounts of all sponsored users will be deactivated, pausing jobs and blocking access to files until the sponsor account is reactivated.

qa_pairs.question: Can a faculty member's deactivated Alliance account be reactivated?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, faculty accounts can be reactivated at any time, even after they have been deactivated.
